chapter_title: Spec. Provisions & Exceptions for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
text:
(a) The governing body of a county or municipality that determines a public highway under its jurisdiction is insufficient to carry a load authorized by Section 622.012 (Axle Weight Restrictions) may prescribe, by order or ordinance, rules governing the operation of a ready-mixed concrete truck over a public highway maintained by the county or municipality.

(b) The rules may include weight limitations on a truck with:

(1) a tandem axle weight that is heavier than 36,000 pounds;

(2) a single axle weight that is heavier than 12,000 pounds; or

(3) a gross weight that is heavier than 48,000 pounds.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 622. Spec. Provisions & Exceptions for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
##### § 622.014 — Local Regulation

(a) The governing body of a county or municipality that determines a public highway under its jurisdiction is insufficient to carry a load authorized by Section 622.012 (Axle Weight Restrictions) may prescribe, by order or ordinance, rules governing the operation of a ready-mixed concrete truck over a public highway maintained by the county or municipality.

(b) The rules may include weight limitations on a truck with:

    (1) a tandem axle weight that is heavier than 36,000 pounds;

    (2) a single axle weight that is heavier than 12,000 pounds; or

    (3) a gross weight that is heavier than 48,000 pounds.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by Acts 2001, 77th Leg., ch. 941, Sec. 31, eff. Sept. 1, 2001.*

subsections.label: b

a tandem axle weight that is heavier than 36,000 pounds;
a single axle weight that is heavier than 12,000 pounds; or
a gross weight that is heavier than 48,000 pounds.
